message:
chore(beads): file 5 modes-of-reasoning gap beads (cc_1)

Applied /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis to crates/frankenterm-core/.
The orchestrator's brief was a single-agent application of the
skill's mode-selection lens, not the full 10-agent swarm. I picked 5
orthogonal perspectives, each on a different taxonomy axis, and
identified one concrete architectural concern per perspective.

ft-dn2tu  [library-vs-binary] storage.rs is 34,097 lines with 20
          public fns — a binary-shaped interior with a library-
          shaped facade. Internal-vs-external surface ratio of
          1705:1. Surgical cut on top of ft-y0loj's broader
          monolith split.

ft-3tvvt  [async-vs-sync] storage.rs has 6+ block_on calls inside
          library code (lines 12215, 23543, 27947, 29831, 30090,
          30460). A library that block_on's is a binary
          masquerading as a library. Deadlock risk on async
          consumers + breaks the cancel-correctness contract the
          README markets.

ft-8nqx0  [audit-vs-operational] 239KB of audit code (recorder_audit
          74KB + recorder_retention 55KB + forensic_export 36KB +
          session_retention 40KB + resize_crash_forensics 34KB)
          physically coupled with live state in the same crate.
          Failure isolation between audit-write and operational-
          state is undefined; cross-cutting Cx cancellation
          aborts BOTH.

ft-bzgxi  [feature-flag-matrix] lib.rs has 24+ #[cfg(feature)]
          gates in the prelude alone; 36+ Cargo features total.
          ft-y0loj's proposal already names the matrix as
          uncitable. CI tests `default` + `--all-features` only;
          single-feature isolation is untested. The fuzz feature
          has 5 known compile errors per the proposal evidence.

ft-k3y0u  [resource-vs-logical-lifecycle] 21+ Drop impls scattered
          across modules with no central ordering contract. Drop
          runs in lexical scope order; semantic ordering (audit
          before storage; replay before recorder; watchdog before
          pane data) is undefined. MEMORY.md's
          asupersync-midflight-cancel-gap note hints at this from
          a different angle; this bead is about Drop ordering
          specifically.

Each bead names a perspective in its title (per the orchestrator's
format) and ties findings to taxonomy axes. None overlap with the
existing audit beads from this session (reality-check, conformance,
golden-artifacts, fuzzing, profiling) — these are architectural
observations, not test-coverage or perf gaps.

Audit ran in <30 minutes. Stopped at 5 findings per cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -335,6 +335,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-jwv4z","title":"[MEDIUM] policy.rs ReadOutput rate-limited but SearchOutput is not — attacker can pivot (88b3a9e9 smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 88b3a9e9 (ft-zb2fl fix): ReadOutput added to rate-limited set; SearchOutput deliberately excluded (policy.rs:137 per test at 5841). An attacker denied ReadOutput can observe the same pane text via SearchOutput. Either rate-limit SearchOutput too (may be noisy for real operators) or document the asymmetry.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:16.829665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:51:09.920979Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:51:09.920707Z","close_reason":"9bae1f47 picks option (b) — document the asymmetry rather than rate-limit SearchOutput. Adds a SECURITY-NOTE block above ActionKind::is_rate_limited() at policy.rs:135-163 with full threat-model rationale, and a docs/security/policy-rate-limit-asymmetry.md writeup covering: TL;DR, threat model (post-auth attacker pivot from ReadOutput-deny to SearchOutput), 4 reasons we accept the pivot (auth is the gate, bandwidth asymmetry favors defender, operator workflow cost, defense-in-depth via redaction + audit + egress), 3 revisit triggers, and implementation guidance against just dropping SearchOutput into the existing match (build a separate is_search_rate_limited gate). Test action_kind_rate_limited strengthened to also assert auth_action() parity between Read/Search and to point readers at the docs before flipping. 19 action_kind/approval/policy tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target. No production behavior change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","review","review-of-review"]}
 {"id":"ft-jz40p","title":"[MEDIUM] TimeSeries::percentile(NaN) returns smallest value instead of None","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/time_series.rs:239` — `TimeSeries::percentile_range` (and the `percentile` convenience that delegates to it).

Same class as ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram::quantile, fixed 2c7b891c). The function relies on `p.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` to validate its input, but `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN (documented Rust behaviour), so a NaN `p` cascades through:

```rust
let p = p.clamp(0.0, 1.0);                          // NaN stays NaN
let idx = ((values.len() - 1) as f64 * p)           // NaN
    .round() as usize;                              // NaN as usize = 0
Some(values[idx])                                   // smallest value
```

A NaN quantile arg silently returns the smallest sample — a plausible-looking number that has no relationship to the requested percentile. Pre-fix the Histogram analog had the same bug; the fix pattern is identical.

## Fix

```rust
if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() {
    return None;
}
```

Matches the ft-b4l62 disposition: NaN `p` is explicitly `None` rather than a silently-wrong small value.

## Tests

* `ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none` — pins None return for NaN p.
* `ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work` — regression: ±inf still clamp to 0.0/1.0 and return min/max samples (pre-existing contract, not broken by the new NaN guard).

## Scope

Single file (`time_series.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane1","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:11:47.750602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756187Z","close_reason":"Fix and both regression tests already landed (time_series.rs:246-254 + :720-745). percentile_range now guards with 'if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() { return None; }' at line 252, so percentile(NaN) returns None instead of silently returning the smallest sample via NaN-clamp-to-0 → (NaN as usize)==0 cascade. Regression tests: ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none (NaN→None on both percentile + percentile_range, plus sanity check that valid p=0.5 still works) and ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work (±inf continue clamping to 0.0/1.0 → min/max sample, pre-existing contract preserved). Verified: 38 time_series:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-k3y0u","title":"[modes-of-reasoning][resource-vs-logical-lifecycle] 21+ Drop impls scattered across modules; cleanup ordering on cancel-mid-flight is undefined","description":"## Perspective: resource lifecycle vs logical lifecycle

Rust's RAII gives every type a Drop hook. A complex async system
has TWO lifecycle clocks:

- **Resource lifecycle** — when does memory/file/socket/lock
  reclamation run? (Drop ordering, lexical scope.)
- **Logical lifecycle** — when does the SESSION/MISSION/Cx
  consider itself finished? (cancellation, completion, error.)

These are NOT the same. A canceled mid-flight Cx triggers Drop
in lexical scope order, but the SEMANTIC ordering (audit-trail
flushed before storage closes; replay capture finalized before
recorder shuts down; semantic-watchdog snapshot taken before
data structures dropped) is undefined.

`crates/frankenterm-core/` has Drop impls in at least:

| File | Drop count |
|------|-----------:|
| `spsc_ring_buffer.rs` | 4 |
| `mcp_tools.rs` | 3 |
| `cancellation_safe_channel.rs` | 2 |
| `telemetry.rs` | 1 |
| `storage.rs` | 1 |
| `snapshot_engine.rs` | 1 |
| `semantic_anomaly_watchdog.rs` | 1 |
| `safe_channel.rs` | 1 |
| `runtime.rs` | 1 |
| `runtime_async.rs` | 1 |

(Total ≥21 visible from grep. Likely more across other modules.)

There is no central registry of Drop ordering, no documented
dependencies between Drop impls, no test that exercises
"cancel-mid-flight at every step of a 50-step workflow and
verify finalization invariants hold." MEMORY.md's
`asupersync-midflight-cancel-gap` note hints at this — recv/
acquire primitives observe pre-cancel but not mid-flight.

## Why this is a real architectural concern

Concrete failure modes this lens predicts:

1. **Audit trail truncated by cancel.** A Cx canceled while
   `recorder_audit::append_entry()` is in flight: the in-memory
   buffer Drop runs in lexical order; if it drops BEFORE the
   storage backend's Drop, the buffer's pending writes are lost.
   No test asserts this doesn't happen.

2. **Snapshot replay diverges by 1 event.** A canceled workflow's
   final "abort" event isn't recorded because the SnapshotEngine
   Drop runs before the workflow Drop (or vice versa). Replay of
   the canceled session shows N-1 events, not N.

3. **Semantic-anomaly-watchdog signature stale.** The watchdog
   maintains a per-pane fingerprint; when the pane Drop runs first,
   the watchdog's last-known signature is for a freed pane, until
   GC reaps it. Brief, but real.

4. **Channel close ordering** — `safe_channel` and
   `cancellation_safe_channel` have different Drop semantics
   (the latter is documented to drain on Drop; the former isn't).
   Whether a sender or receiver Drop happens first determines
   whether the receiver sees `Closed` vs blocks forever — that
   ordering is implicit and can flip with refactoring.

The skill's resource/logical-lifecycle lens is exactly what makes
these visible. From inside a single Drop impl, everything looks
correct; from the system view, the graph of inter-Drop dependencies
is undocumented.

## Suggested approach

1. **Drop dependency graph** — author
   `docs/drop-ordering-contract.md` listing every Drop impl in
   `frankenterm-core` and its required predecessors (e.g.,
   "RecorderAudit::Drop must complete before Storage::Drop").

2. **Define a session shutdown protocol** — a `SessionShutdown`
   trait + `shutdown(Cx) -> impl Future<Output=()>` async method
   on the top-level lifecycle types. Drop becomes the FALLBACK
   (best-effort cleanup); explicit `shutdown(Cx)` is the primary
   path.

3. **Test cancel-mid-flight invariants** —
   `tests/cancel_invariants_at_each_step.rs`: drive a 50-step
   workflow, cancel at each step boundary, assert:
   - Audit trail records the cancel event.
   - Recorder events are flushed.
   - Replay of the canceled session yields exactly the events
     that completed before cancel.

4. **Document the lexical-scope Drop pattern as fallback only**
   — in AGENTS.md or a sibling doc, a "Drop is best-effort,
   shutdown(Cx) is the contract" rule.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring every Drop into shutdown() (large, multi-PR work).
- The asupersync runtime itself (it's load-bearing infrastructure
  per AGENTS.md).
- The cancel-mid-flight asymmetry MEMORY.md note tracks
  separately (different angle: this bead is about Drop ordering,
  that note is about primitive observability of the cancel
  signal).

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/drop-ordering-contract.md` lists every Drop in
  `frankenterm-core`.
- A `SessionShutdown` trait + at least one consumer (recorder,
  storage, or snapshot engine) prototype exists.
- `tests/cancel_invariants_at_each_step.rs` exists with at least
  10 step boundaries and 3 invariant assertions per step.
- AGENTS.md gains a rule "Drop is best-effort; explicit shutdown
  is the contract".

## References

- `grep -rcE "impl Drop for " crates/frankenterm-core/src/*.rs`
  (the 21+ Drop impls)
- MEMORY.md `asupersync-midflight-cancel-gap` (related but
  different lens)
- README §Platform Model (cancel-correctness contract that
  this lens shows is incompletely realized at the Drop level)
- Skill: modes-of-reasoning — resource/logical-lifecycle is
  a "different axes" disagreement-resolution case, often
  invisible from inside any single module

Filed by cc_1 modes-of-reasoning audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:35.468408Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:35.468408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-k542h","title":"[MEDIUM] v0 schema init runs outside a transaction — crash mid-migration leaves half-repaired DB (c06b230a follow-up)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of c06b230a: repair_existing_v0_tables_before_schema_sql + SCHEMA_SQL + run_migrations(0) run sequentially without a transaction wrapper. A crash between repair and run_migrations leaves partially-applied ALTERs with user_version unchanged; next open re-triggers repair on half-repaired tables. Fix: wrap the triple in a single BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT. storage.rs ~line 4440.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cc7","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:16.123823Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:01:29.474685Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:01:29.474281Z","close_reason":"wrap v0 init triple in BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT (storage.rs); fault-injection unit test asserts atomic rollback (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","review-of-review","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-k6jco","title":"[ft-kuxho/B] Design CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED window for additive PDU rolling upgrade","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:57:50.388575Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:29.470865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:29.470589Z","close_reason":"design doc landed at docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md; 3 child beads filed (ft-kuxho.{1,2,3} — auto-numbered, titles preserve the ft-kuxho.B.{1,2,3} tag) covering the 3-step rollout (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","codec","rfc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-k6jco","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:58:10.202673Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kbbc3","title":"[tx][integrity][P1] wa.tx_run evaluates synthetic (allow-all + always-success) inputs and persists fabricated Committed state to the tx contract file","description":"## Finding

The production MCP tool `wa.tx_run` at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:2985-3186` runs its prepare/commit/compensate pipeline on **synthetic inputs**, not on real executor outputs. It then persists the synthetic-success results to the tx contract file on disk.

### Call sequence

1. `wa.tx_run` handler loads the MissionTxContract from disk.
2. `let gate_inputs = mcp_build_tx_prepare_gate_inputs(&contract);` at :3089. Under the alias in mcp.rs:56 this resolves to `crate::plan::tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all(contract)` — a helper at plan.rs:3438 that hardcodes `policy_passed: true`, `reservation_available: true`, `approval_satisfied: true`, `target_liveness: true` for every step.
3. `evaluate_prepare_phase` is fed those all-true inputs and returns AllReady.
4. `let commit_inputs = mcp_build_tx_commit_step_inputs(...)` at :3121 resolves to `mission_tx_commit_step_inputs` at plan.rs:3571, which hardcodes `success: true` for every step unless the caller explicitly passes `fail_step`.
5. Same for compensation.
6. The resulting `TxCommitReport` / `TxCompensationReport` is written back to the contract file via `mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path` at :3186 with `lifecycle_state` set to the target (typically Committed) and receipts populated.

### Impact

- The MCP tool description at :2990 says "Execute deterministic tx prepare+commit and compensation on partial failure (robot parity)" — operators reasonably infer real execution.
- A peer agent invoking `wa.tx_run` on a contract with (e.g.) a step that SHOULD fail policy gating will see the contract transition to Committed on disk without any real policy check having fired.
- A downstream tool that queries the tx contract file (`wa.tx_show --include-contract`, operator dashboards, audit exports) will see a fabricated success state. The audit log will record the transitions without any runtime evidence that the underlying steps happened.
- The REAL executor infrastructure (`PaneStepExecutor` at tx_execution.rs:234, `TxExecutionEngine` at :731) is grep-verified to NOT be called from the MCP path (zero matches for either type in mcp_tools.rs). They exist but are unused by wa.tx_run.

### Related beads (context)

- ft-bekwl + ft-npj7t (both closed) fixed a parallel 'synthetic success in compensation' path in `PaneStepExecutor`. This bead is DIFFERENT: the MCP handler doesn't use `PaneStepExecutor` at all.
- ft-73f9r added an e2e test for wa.tx plan/run/rollback. That test validates deterministic-synthetic behavior, not real-executor behavior — so it doesn't catch this gap.

### Fix direction

The correct call sequence for `wa.tx_run` is:
1. Build a real `PaneStepExecutor` (needs wezterm handle, reservation manager, policy engine with rate limiter, storage).
2. Call `PaneStepExecutor::evaluate_gates(contract, now_ms)` for real prepare inputs.
3. Feed those to `evaluate_prepare_phase`.
4. If prepare passes, call `PaneStepExecutor::execute_steps(contract, fail_step, now_ms)` for real commit inputs.
5. Feed those to `execute_commit_phase`.
6. If commit fails partially, call `PaneStepExecutor::execute_compensations(contract, &commit, fail_for_step, now_ms)` and feed to `execute_compensation_phase`.

This is a substantial refactor: the MCP handler currently has neither the wezterm handle, reservation manager, nor policy engine in the right shape. Approximate size: ~150-200 LoC + per-step executor wiring.

### Interim mitigation

Until the real executor is wired, `wa.tx_run` should either:
(a) Return a clear 'simulation' marker in the envelope (e.g., `data.mode: "synthetic_preview"`) and refuse to persist lifecycle_state changes back to the contract file, OR
(b) Be gated behind an explicit `--simulate` flag and error when called without it.

(a) is the smaller diff and preserves current e2e tests. (b) is more operator-clear but breaks the tool's default behavior.

### Severity rationale

P1 (not P0):
- No direct remote exploitation path: MCP ships over stdio/localhost by default and `wa.tx_run` is already gated by `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation` per ft-x86z2 (so it's behind policy auth).
- But the persisted synthetic-success state on disk is a real data-integrity hazard for any operator reading the tx contract file as authoritative.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /multi-pass-bug-hunting pass on tx subsystem, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:50:42.857222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:06:26.672249Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:06:26.671995Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b7b44081: wa.tx_run now goes through real executor (no synthetic allow-all)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","integrity","mcp","security","tx"]}
